---
title: 'Promotion- Going up^'
date: '2010-06-01T13:47:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3478861642027163007'
blogger_filename: '/2010/06/promotion-going-up.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
---

Review of Pastor George's Series on Promotin:<br /><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/goingup-ss-824e6cde9467.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/goingup-ss-bdb338ead998.jpg" /></a></div>Sunday 8:30 AM 4/11/2010<br />Pastor George Pearsons<br /><b>Going Up: Godâ€™s  System of Promotionâ€”Part 1</b>           <br /><b>Three Vital Keys to Your Promotion</b><br /><br />Started on Sundays 04/11 through 05/30 2010<br /><br />The three keys to promotion in God are:<br /><ul><li>Promotion comes from God</li><ul><li>You don't have to run after promotion, promotion will chase YOU down. </li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Psalm 75:4-7&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-15076">4</sup> To the  arrogant I say, 'Boast no more,' <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; and to the wicked, 'Do not  lift up your horns. </span><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-15077">5</sup>  Do not lift your horns against heaven; <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; do not speak with  outstretched neck.' " </span><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-15078">6</sup>  No one from the east or the west <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; or from the desert can  exalt a man. </span><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-15079">7</sup> But  it is God who judges: <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; He brings one down, he exalts another.</span></li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">1 Corinthians 12:18&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><span style="font-size: x-small;"><sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-28637">18</sup>But in fact God has  arranged the parts in the body, every one of them, just as he wanted  them to be.</span></li></ul><li>Promotion starts with the heart</li><ul><li>David never said "I WILL become King!" He simply served Saul faithfully despite the fact that Saul tried to kill him.&nbsp; </li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Acts 13:22&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><span style="font-size: x-small;"><sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-27373">22</sup>After removing Saul,  he made David their king. He testified concerning him: 'I have found  David son of Jesse a man after my own heart; he will do everything I  want him to do.'</span><br />&nbsp;</li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;1 Samuel 16:7&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-7603">7</sup> But the LORD  said to Samuel, "Do not consider his appearance or his height, for I  have rejected him. The LORD does not look at the things man looks at.  Man looks at the outward appearance, but the LORD looks at the heart."</span><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;</span><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;"> </span></li></ul><li>Promotion is NOT a goal it is a result!</li><ul><li>David Reaped the Harvest of Promotion </li><li>Promotion is not something that we seek it is a harvest that we reap</li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Matthew 6:33&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><span style="font-size: x-small;"><sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-23316">33</sup>But seek first his  kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you  as well.</span></li></ul></ul>There are several Character Issues regarding Promotion. If your character is not right any promotion will not last. What you compromise to get you WILL eventually loose.<br /><br />The Character issues are:<br /><br /><ul><li>Servants Heart</li><ul><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Psalm 116:16&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-15865">16</sup> O LORD,  truly I am your servant; <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; I am your servant, the son of your  maidservant <sup class="footnote" value="[&lt;a  href=&quot;#fen-NIV-15865a&quot; title=&quot;See footnote  a&quot;&gt;a&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm%20116:16&amp;version=NIV#fen-NIV-15865a" title="See footnote a">a</a>]</sup> ; <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; you have freed me from  my chains.</span></li><li> <h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Luke 22:27&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><span style="font-size: x-small;"><sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-25883">27</sup>For who is greater,  the one who is at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one who  is at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.</span></li></ul><li>Humility</li><ul><li>Humility is NOT "You put You Down"</li><li>Humility IS "You Put You Aside for others"</li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Proverbs 29:23&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-17248">23</sup> A man's  pride brings him low, <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; but a man of lowly spirit gains honor.</span><br /><br /><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;</span></li></ul><li>Diligence</li><ul><li><h4><span style="font-size: x-small;">1 Samuel 17</span></h4><span style="font-size: x-small;">The three oldest followed Saul, <sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-7634">15</sup> but David went back and forth from Saul to tend his father's sheep at  Bethlehem... </span><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;"><sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-7676">57</sup> As soon as David  returned from killing the Philistine, Abner took him and brought him  before Saul, with David still holding the Philistine's head. </span><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-7677">58</sup> "Whose son are you, young  man?" Saul asked him. <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;David said, "I am the son of your  servant Jesse of Bethlehem."</span></li><li> <h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Proverbs 12:24&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-16744">24</sup> Diligent  hands will rule, <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; but laziness ends in slave labor.</span></li></ul><li>Faithfulness</li><ul><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Proverbs 20:6&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-16961">6</sup> Many a man  claims to have unfailing love, <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; but a faithful man who can  find?</span></li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">1 Samuel 22:14&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-7802">14</sup> Ahimelech  answered the king, "Who of all your servants is as loyal as David, the  king's son-in-law, captain of your bodyguard and highly respected in  your household?</span></li><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Matthew 25:21&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-24027">21</sup>"His master  replied, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful  with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Come and  share your master's happiness!'</span></li></ul><li>Integrity</li><ul><li><h2 id="passage_heading"><span style="font-size: x-small;">Psalm 26:11&nbsp;(New International Version)</span></h2><br /><span style="font-size: x-small;">&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NIV-14285">11</sup> But I lead a  blameless life; <br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; redeem me and be merciful to me.</span></li></ul></ul><b> </b><br /><b> </b><br /><b> </b><br /><b>Not pursueing God and His kingdom will cause you to be held back in life. The things that you think matter here matter little in God's eyes. There will be many who were held in HIGH esteem by our christian histories that occupy the lowest ranks of Heaven. Don't set your eyes on being approved of men but of God.&nbsp;</b><br /><br /><b>Don't fall into pride on the other side either which is false humility. Don't seek to put yourself down out of the sight of men if that is your calling. Just because you are are noticed of men doesn't mean you won't have a high rank in the Kingdom.&nbsp;</b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b>God's kingdom doesn't care if men do or don't see you. God's kingdom is concerned with how God sees you. The hand of the Diligent will rule.&nbsp;</b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b>DW</b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><br /></b><br /><b><iframe align="left" frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="http://rcm.amazon.com/e/cm?t=your0f4d-20&amp;o=1&amp;p=8&amp;l=bpl&amp;asins=192937190X&amp;fc1=000000&amp;IS2=1&amp;lt1=_blank&amp;m=amazon&amp;lc1=0000FF&amp;bc1=000000&amp;bg1=FFFFFF&amp;f=ifr" style="height: 245px; padding-right: 10px; padding-top: 5px; width: 131px;"></iframe><a href="http://www.bvov.tv/emic/ondemand.php">http://www.bvov.tv/emic/ondemand.php</a></b><br /><b> </b>
